JWT-based auth pipeline: sign, verify, and enforce credentials (ADR-018 §4).\\n//!\\n//! # Verification order\\n//!\\n//! 1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` → `Unauthenticated` if missing.\\n//! 2. Parse + verify HS256 signature → `CredentialInvalid` / `CredentialMalformed`.\\n//! 3. Check `iss` against issuer ID → `CredentialForeignIssuer`.\\n//! 4. Check `exp`/`nbf` with ±30 s skew → `CredentialExpired` / `CredentialNotYetValid`.\\n//! 5. Check `jti` revocation (cache → storage) → `CredentialRevoked`.\\n//! 6. Compare `aud` to URL tenant → `CredentialWrongTenant`.\\n//! 7. Look up `sub` in users table, check suspension → `UserSuspended`.\\n//! 8. Check tenant membership → `NotATenantMember`.\\n//! 9. Walk grants for URL database + HTTP-method-derived op → `DatabaseNotGranted` / `OpNotGranted`.\\n//!\\n//! On success, installs `ResolvedIdentity` into the request extension.\\n//! On failure, returns a JSON error response built from `AuthError::status_code` and\\n//! `AuthError::error_code`.\\n\\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex, RwLock};\\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\\n\\nuse axum::extract::State;\\nuse axum::middleware::Next;\\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\\nuse http::{HeaderMap, Method, StatusCode};\\nuse jsonwebtoken::{Algorithm, DecodingKey, EncodingKey, Header, Validation, decode, encode};\\nuse serde_json::json;\\nuse uuid::Uuid;\\n\\nuse axon_core::auth::{AuthError, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, UserId};\\nuse axon_storage::adapter::StorageAdapter;\\n\\n// ── JwtIssuer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Signs and verifies JWTs using HS256.\\npub struct JwtIssuer {\\n    secret: Vec\u003cu8\u003e,\\n    /// Issuer identifier placed in the `iss` claim and verified on every request.\\n    pub issuer_id: String,\\n}\\n\\nimpl JwtIssuer {\\n    /// Create a new issuer with the given HMAC secret and issuer ID.\\n    pub fn new(secret: Vec\u003cu8\u003e, issuer_id: String) -\u003e Self {\\n        Self { secret, issuer_id }\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Sign `claims` into a compact HS256 JWT string.\\n    pub fn issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e {\\n        encode(\\n            \u0026Header::new(Algorithm::HS256),\\n            claims,\\n            \u0026EncodingKey::from_secret(\u0026self.secret),\\n        )\\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Verify the signature and deserialise the claims.\\n    ///\\n    /// Time-based checks (`exp`, `nbf`) are intentionally skipped here; the\\n    /// middleware performs them manually with a configurable skew budget.\\n    pub fn verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e {\\n        let mut validation = Validation::new(Algorithm::HS256);\\n        validation.validate_exp = false;\\n        validation.validate_nbf = false;\\n        validation.validate_aud = false;\\n        validation.required_spec_claims = HashSet::new();\\n\\n        decode::\u003cJwtClaims\u003e(token, \u0026DecodingKey::from_secret(\u0026self.secret), \u0026validation)\\n            .map(|td| td.claims)\\n            .map_err(|e| match e.kind() {\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidSignature =\u003e AuthError::CredentialInvalid,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Json(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Base64(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Utf8(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidToken =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                _ =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n            })\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── InMemoryRevocationCache ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// In-process cache of revoked JWT IDs.\\n///\\n/// The middleware checks this first before falling back to the storage adapter,\\n/// avoiding a round-trip to storage on every request for known-revoked tokens.\\n#[derive(Default)]\\npub struct InMemoryRevocationCache {\\n    inner: RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e,\\n}\\n\\nimpl InMemoryRevocationCache {\\n    /// Create an empty cache.\\n    pub fn new() -\u003e Self {\\n        Self::default()\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Returns `true` if `jti` is in the cache.\\n    pub fn contains(\u0026self, jti: \u0026Uuid) -\u003e bool {\\n        self.inner\\n            .read()\\n            .map(|g| g.contains(jti))\\n            .unwrap_or(false)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Add `jti` to the cache.\\n    pub fn insert(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) {\\n        if let Ok(mut g) = self.inner.write() {\\n            g.insert(jti);\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── AuthPipelineState ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Shared state for the JWT verification middleware.\\n///\\n/// Wrap in `Arc` before passing to `axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state`.\\n#[derive(Clone)]\\npub struct AuthPipelineState {\\n    /// Token issuer / verifier.\\n    pub issuer: Arc\u003cJwtIssuer\u003e,\\n    /// Fast in-process revocation check.\\n    pub revocation_cache: Arc\u003cInMemoryRevocationCache\u003e,\\n    /// Authoritative storage for user, membership, and revocation data.\\n    pub storage: Arc\u003cMutex\u003cBox\u003cdyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e\u003e\u003e,\\n}\\n\\nimpl AuthPipelineState {\\n    /// Convenience constructor.\\n    pub fn new(issuer: JwtIssuer, storage: Box\u003cdyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e) -\u003e Self {\\n        Self {\\n            issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\\n            revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n            storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Middleware ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Axum middleware that enforces the ADR-018 §4 JWT verification order.\\n///\\n/// Install with:\\n/// ```ignore\\n/// Router::new()\\n///     .route(\\\"/tenants/:t/databases/:db/…\\\", …)\\n///     .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n///         Arc::new(state),\\n///         jwt_verify_layer,\\n///     ))\\n/// ```\\npub async fn jwt_verify_layer(\\n    State(state): State\u003cArc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e\u003e,\\n    request: axum::extract::Request,\\n    next: Next,\\n) -\u003e Response {\\n    let path = request.uri().path().to_string();\\n\\n    // Skip middleware for paths that are not data-plane paths.\\n    let Some((tenant, database)) = extract_tenant_database(\u0026path) else {\\n        return next.run(request).await;\\n    };\\n\\n    let tenant = tenant.to_string();\\n    let database = database.to_string();\\n    let method = request.method().clone();\\n    let headers = request.headers().clone();\\n\\n    match verify_request(\u0026state, \u0026headers, \u0026method, \u0026tenant, \u0026database) {\\n        Ok(identity) =\u003e {\\n            let mut request = request;\\n            request.extensions_mut().insert(identity);\\n            next.run(request).await\\n        }\\n        Err(err) =\u003e AuthErrorResponse(err).into_response(),\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Core verification logic ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nfn verify_request(\\n    state: \u0026AuthPipelineState,\\n    headers: \u0026HeaderMap,\\n    method: \u0026Method,\\n    tenant: \u0026str,\\n    database: \u0026str,\\n) -\u003e Result\u003cResolvedIdentity, AuthError\u003e {\\n    // Step 1: Extract Bearer token.\\n    let token = extract_bearer_token(headers)?;\\n\\n    // Step 2: Verify JWT signature and structure.\\n    let claims = state.issuer.verify(token)?;\\n\\n    // Step 3: Check iss.\\n    if claims.iss != state.issuer.issuer_id {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialForeignIssuer);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 4: Check exp/nbf with 30 s skew.\\n    let now = SystemTime::now()\\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n        .unwrap_or_default()\\n        .as_secs();\\n    const SKEW: u64 = 30;\\n    // Expired: exp + skew \u003c now  ↔  expired more than `skew` seconds ago\\n    if claims.exp.saturating_add(SKEW) \u003c now {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialExpired);\\n    }\\n    // Not yet valid: nbf \u003e now + skew  ↔  valid more than `skew` seconds from now\\n    if claims.nbf \u003e now.saturating_add(SKEW) {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialNotYetValid);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 5: Check JTI revocation (cache first, then storage).\\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims\\n        .jti\\n        .parse()\\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\\n    if state.revocation_cache.contains(\u0026jti_uuid) {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\\n    }\\n    {\\n        let storage = state\\n            .storage\\n            .lock()\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n        if storage\\n            .is_jti_revoked(jti_uuid)\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?\\n        {\\n            // Populate the cache so subsequent checks avoid another storage round-trip.\\n            drop(storage);\\n            state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\\n            return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 6: Compare aud to URL tenant.\\n    if claims.aud != tenant {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialWrongTenant);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 7: Look up user, check suspension.\\n    let user_id = UserId::new(\u0026claims.sub);\\n    let user = {\\n        let storage = state\\n            .storage\\n            .lock()\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n        storage\\n            .get_user(user_id.clone())\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::UserSuspended)?\\n            .ok_or(AuthError::UserSuspended)?\\n    };\\n    if user.suspended_at_ms.is_some() {\\n        return Err(AuthError::UserSuspended);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 8: Check tenant membership.\\n    let tenant_id = TenantId::new(tenant);\\n    {\\n        let storage = state\\n            .storage\\n            .lock()\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n        storage\\n            .get_tenant_member(tenant_id.clone(), user_id.clone())\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::NotATenantMember)?\\n            .ok_or(AuthError::NotATenantMember)?;\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 9: Walk grants for the URL database + HTTP-method-derived op.\\n    let granted_db = claims\\n        .grants\\n        .find_database(database)\\n        .ok_or(AuthError::DatabaseNotGranted)?;\\n\\n    let required_op = if method == Method::GET {\\n        Op::Read\\n    } else {\\n        Op::Write\\n    };\\n    if !granted_db.has_op(required_op) {\\n        return Err(AuthError::OpNotGranted);\\n    }\\n\\n    Ok(ResolvedIdentity {\\n        user_id,\\n        tenant_id,\\n        grants: claims.grants,\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n// ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Extract the `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` value from the `Authorization` header.\\nfn extract_bearer_token(headers: \u0026HeaderMap) -\u003e Result\u003c\u0026str, AuthError\u003e {\\n    let auth_header = headers\\n        .get(http::header::AUTHORIZATION)\\n        .ok_or(AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n\\n    let auth_str = auth_header\\n        .to_str()\\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\\n\\n    auth_str\\n        .strip_prefix(\\\"Bearer \\\")\\n        .ok_or(AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\\n}\\n\\n/// Extract `(tenant, database)` from a data-plane URL path.\\n///\\n/// Returns `Some((tenant, database))` for paths matching\\n/// `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/…`, `None` otherwise.\\n/// Non-data-plane paths skip the middleware entirely.\\npub fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e {\\n    let path = path.strip_prefix('/')?;\\n    let mut parts = path.splitn(5, '/');\\n    match (parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next()) {\\n        (Some(\\\"tenants\\\"), Some(tenant), Some(\\\"databases\\\"), Some(rest))\\n            if !tenant.is_empty() \u0026\u0026 !rest.is_empty() =\u003e\\n        {\\n            let database = rest.split('/').next()?;\\n            if database.is_empty() {\\n                None\\n            } else {\\n                Some((tenant, database))\\n            }\\n        }\\n        _ =\u003e None,\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── AuthError → axum Response ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Newtype wrapper that lets us implement `IntoResponse` for `AuthError` in\\n/// this crate (orphan-rule: neither `IntoResponse` nor `AuthError` is local).\\npub struct AuthErrorResponse(pub AuthError);\\n\\nimpl IntoResponse for AuthErrorResponse {\\n    fn into_response(self) -\u003e Response {\\n        let status = StatusCode::from_u16(self.0.status_code())\\n            .unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);\\n        let body = json!({\\n            \\\"error\\\": {\\n                \\\"code\\\": self.0.error_code(),\\n                \\\"message\\\": self.0.to_string()\\n            }\\n        });\\n        (status, axum::Json(body)).into_response()\\n TENANT.to_string(),\\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\\n        iat: now,\\n        nbf: now,\\n        exp: now + 3600,\\n        grants: Grants {\\n            databases: Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\\n .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n            state,\\n            jwt_verify_layer,\\n        ))\\n}\\n\\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request\u003cBody\u003e) -\u003e (StatusCode, directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\\n\\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\\n\\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\\n\\n## When to Use\\n\\n- After implementing a tracked issue\\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\\n\\n## What It Does\\n\\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\\n2. **Format** commit message:\\n   - First line: `\u003cissue-id\u003e \u003cconcise summary\u003e` (under 72 chars)\\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\\n\\n## Commit Message Format\\n\\n```\\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\\n\\nLonger description of what changed and why. data.text: I found the repo’s sync machinery. I’m reading the copy-skills path now because it determines whether I should touch just the source tree or keep the embedded/plugin mirror in sync as part of the same commit.

data.output:
@echo "Verifying Go discovery excludes frontend node_modules..."
	@cd . && if go list ./... | grep -q 'frontend/node_modules'; then \
		echo "go list discovered packages under internal/server/frontend/node_modules"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi

# Sync the canonical default-plugin skill tree (../library/skills/) and its
# manifest into internal/registry/defaultplugin/library/ so //go:embed (which
# cannot traverse upward out of the package) picks them up. Runs as a prereq
# of build/test/dev so contributors never see a stale embedded default
# package. rsync --delete keeps the skill destination in exact sync.
DEFAULT_PKG_SRC := ../library
DEFAULT_PKG_DST := internal/registry/defaultplugin/library
copy-skills:
	@if [ ! -d "$(DEFAULT_PKG_SRC)/skills" ]; then \
		echo "error: $(DEFAULT_PKG_SRC)/skills not found (run from cli/)"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@rsync -a --delete $(DEFAULT_PKG_SRC)/skills/ $(DEFAULT_PKG_DST)/skills/
	@cp $(DEFAULT_PKG_SRC)/package.yaml $(DEFAULT_PKG_DST)/package.yaml

# Build for current platform
build: copy-skills frontend-build frontend-verify
	@echo "Building DDx CLI..."
	@mkdir -p $(BUILD_DIR)
	go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx .

# Build for all platforms
build-all: clean deps copy-skills
	@echo "Building for all platforms..."
	@mkdir -p $(BUILD_DIR)
	@echo "Building current platform..."
	go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx .
	@for platform in $(PLATFORMS); do \
		OS=$$(echo $$platform | cut -d'/' -f1); \
		ARCH=$$(echo $$platform | cut -d'/' -f2); \
		echo "Building $$OS/$$ARCH..."; \
		GOOS=$$OS GOARCH=$$ARCH go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx-$$OS-$$ARCH .; \
		if [ $$OS = "windows" ]; then \
			mv $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx-$$OS-$$ARCH $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx-$$OS-$$ARCH.exe; \
		fi; \
	done

# Install dependencies
deps:
	@echo "Installing dependencies..."
	go mod download
	go mod tidy

# Run tests
test: copy-skills go-discovery-verify
	@echo "Running tests..."

fi
      fail_text: "DDx validation failed. Run 'ddx doctor' for details"

    skill-schema:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Validate skill metadata"
      run: |
        if ! git diff --cached --name-only | grep -qE '^(library/skills/[^/]+/SKILL\.md|cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library/skills/[^/]+/SKILL\.md)$'; then
          exit 0
        fi
        make skill-schema
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase

    sync-embedded-default-plugin:
      name: "Sync cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library from library/"
      root: "cli/"
      run: |
        if ! git diff --cached --name-only | grep -qE '^library/(skills/|package\.yaml$)'; then
          exit 0
        fi
        make copy-skills
        cd .. && git add cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library/
      stage_fixed: true
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase
      fail_text: "Failed to sync cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library from library/. Run 'cd cli && make copy-skills' manually and stage the result."

    design-md-lint:
      name: "DESIGN.md lint"
      glob: ".stitch/DESIGN.md"
      run: |
        if ! command -v bun >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          echo "bun not found; skipping DESIGN.md lint"

data.output: CLAUDE.md:221:Plugin and persona lookup follows project > global > baked-in precedence (`ddx-937de9fc`): project-local (`ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/`, which is `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/` in-tree or `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<identity>/plugins/<name>/` in convention mode) → global install (`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/`) → baked-in binary default (only for the `ddx` plugin). The project layer always wins when present. `ddx doctor` reports both the global install layer and the project install layer, including when the project copy is absent and falls through to the global layer (`lazy-resolves-to-global`). `ddx install <name> --global` is reinstated under the zero-footprint epic for machine-wide installs (skills land in `~/.claude/skills/` and `~/.agents/skills/`); omit `--global` for per-project installs. See `docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md` for the full decision record.

1	---
     2	name: ddx
     3	description: Operates the DDx toolkit for document-driven development. Covers beads (work items), the queue, executions, agents, harnesses, personas, reviews, spec-id. Use when the user says "do work", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "execute a bead", "review this", "review with fresh eyes", "fold in this guidance", "review again", "break this down into specs and beads", "make this testable", "check against spec", "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "what's blocking the queue", "create a bead", "file this as work", "run an agent", "dispatch", "use a persona", "how am I doing", "ddx doctor", or mentions any ddx CLI command.
     4	---
     5	
     6	# DDx
     7	
     8	DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is a CLI platform for
     9	document-driven development. It ships a bead tracker (portable work
    10	items with acceptance criteria), a task-execution boundary (DDx
    11	forwards raw passthrough constraints for harness, provider, model,
    12	and profile while power travels as `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds;
    13	Fizeau owns concrete routing, provider/model discovery, aliases,
    14	fuzzy matching, catalog lookups, and transcript/session rendering), a
    15	persona system (bindable AI personalities), a library registry
    16	(plugins with prompts, templates, personas), and git-aware
    17	synchronization. This skill makes any skills-compatible coding agent
    18	(Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) understand and operate
    19	the DDx surface correctly.
    20	
    21	## How this skill works
    22	
    23	The skill body you're reading is an **overview** plus an **intent
    24	router**. The real domain guidance lives in `reference/*.md` files.
    25	
    26	Install locations: this skill is resolved from the project tier
    27	(`ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx/`, which is `<project>/.ddx/plugins/ddx/` in-tree
    28	or `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<identity>/plugins/ddx/` in convention
    29	mode), the global machine layer (`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/`),
    30	or the binary's baked-in default — see **Install topology** below. `ddx doctor`
    31	reports which layer is active.
    32	
    33	**Directive: before responding to any DDx-related request, read the
    34	matching reference file from the router table below. The router is
    35	not optional — your answer must be grounded in the reference file's
    36	guidance, not this overview alone.**
    37	
    38	## Install topology
    39	
    40	DDx resolves the default `ddx` package in three layers: the project-local
    41	tier at `ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx/` (in-tree or convention mode), then
    42	the global install at `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/`, then the
    43	baked-in package embedded in the binary. This is project > global > baked-in precedence.
    44	`ddx doctor` reports the project and global layers separately so operators
    45	can tell whether the project copy is real, missing, or lazily resolving to
    46	the global layer.
    47	
    48	For project installs, the agent-facing skill outputs live in
    49	`<project>/.agents/skills/<name>/` and `<project>/.claude/skills/<name>/`.
    50	For machine-wide installs (`ddx install <name> --global`), the plugin lands in
    51	`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/` and skill links are created under
    52	`~/.agents/skills/<name>/` and `~/.claude/skills/<name>/`. `ddx install
    53	--global` is reinstated under the zero-footprint epic; unmanaged home-directory
    54	skill placements outside these managed paths are retired.
    55	
    56	## Vocabulary
    57	
    58	Single source of truth for DDx terms. Every reference file uses these
    59	exact definitions.
    60	
    61	- **Bead** — a portable work item (task, bug, epic, chore) with
    62	  metadata, dependencies, and acceptance criteria. `ddx bead create`.
    63	- **Queue** — the set of active beads. The *execution-ready queue* is
    64	  `status=open` work whose dependencies are closed and which is not parked by
    65	  cooldown, supersession, or execution-eligibility policy. `ddx bead ready`,
    66	  `ddx bead status`.
    67	- **Lifecycle status** — the persisted bead state: `proposed`, `open`,
    68	  `in_progress`, `blocked`, `closed`, or `cancelled`.
    69	- **Ready** — a bead whose dependencies are all closed and which is
    70	  eligible to be picked up next. `ddx bead ready`.
    71	- **Blocked** — `status=blocked` work paused by a hard external recheckable
    72	  blocker. Ordinary dependency waits are derived queue state, not blocked
    73	  status. `ddx bead blocked`.
    74	- **Proposed** — work awaiting operator decision before autonomous execution.
    75	  Proposed beads are not worker-eligible until accepted, rewritten, split,
    76	  blocked externally, cancelled, or otherwise resolved.
    77	- **Claim** — mark a bead as in-progress by an agent (concurrent-write
    78	  protection). `ddx bead update <id> --claim`.
    79	- **Close** — mark a bead as done, with evidence (session, commit
    80	  SHA). `ddx bead close <id>`. Beads only close on execution outcomes

722371 /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/codex exec --json --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox -C /home/erik/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-363e875f-20260702T221915-ece25de1 -m gpt-5.4-mini <execute-bead>   <instructions> You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. Use `parent=<parent-id>` metadata and wire `parent -> child`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.   </instructions>   <bead id="ddx-363e875f">     <title>config: TestModeMatrix — both modes pass the same scenario suite, spec: part 4</title>     <description> PROBLEM

The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. Use `parent=<parent-id>` metadata and wire `parent -> child`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

data.text: I’ve decided the long single-run `internal/agent` job isn’t a good use of time or CPU while I’m sharding the suite. I’m stopping that detached job so I can replace it with narrower test groups that complete within the session constraints.

I’m switching the large package trees to test-name shards. First shard is the `cmd` tests whose names start with `A` through `C`; if that completes within the session limit, I’ll continue through the remaining ranges.
The first `cmd` shard is running. I’m polling it once before deciding whether the shard size is acceptable or needs to be cut finer.
The shard is still running, which is fine so far. I’m checking whether it’s active CPU work or just a stalled wrapper before I decide if this shard needs to be split again.

data.text: I hit a quoting snag in the process-state probe, so I’m falling back to the shard itself. It’s still running, and I’m giving it a bit more time before I decide whether to keep the same grouping.
The first shard is past the one-minute mark without failing, which is a better sign than the monolithic run. I’m letting it keep going rather than chopping it prematurely.

package cmd

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"runtime"
	"strings"
	"syscall"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

type cleanupCommandJSON struct {
	DryRun                      bool             `json:"dry_run"`
	ProjectRoot                 string           `json:"project_root"`
	TempRoot                    string           `json:"temp_root"`
	ScannedTempDirs             int              `json:"scanned_temp_dirs"`
	ScannedEvidenceDirs         int              `json:"scanned_evidence_dirs"`
	CompleteEvidenceDirs        int              `json:"complete_evidence_dirs"`
	ScannedScratchDirs          int              `json:"scanned_scratch_dirs"`
	RemovedUnregisteredTempDirs int64            `json:"removed_unregistered_temp_dirs"`
	RemovedRegisteredWorktrees  int64            `json:"removed_registered_worktrees"`
	RemovedRunStateFiles        int64            `json:"removed_run_state_files"`
	RemovedScratchDirs          int64            `json:"removed_scratch_dirs"`
	PreservedActiveScratchDirs  int64            `json:"preserved_active_scratch_dirs"`
	BytesReclaimed              int64            `json:"bytes_reclaimed"`
	InodesReclaimed             int64            `json:"inodes_reclaimed"`
	ScratchBytesReclaimed       int64            `json:"scratch_bytes_reclaimed"`
	ScratchInodesReclaimed      int64            `json:"scratch_inodes_reclaimed"`
	Warnings                    []map[string]any `json:"warnings"`
	BlockedErrors               []map[string]any `json:"blocked_errors"`
	Observations                []map[string]any `json:"observations"`
	ProcessFindingsCount        int              `json:"process_findings_count"`
	StaleProcessGroups          int              `json:"stale_process_groups"`
	TerminatedProcessGroups     int              `json:"terminated_process_groups"`
	ProcessFindings             []map[string]any `json:"process_findings"`
}

func setupCleanupCommandProject(t *testing.T) (string, string) {
	t.Helper()
	projectRoot := t.TempDir()
	tempParent := t.TempDir()
	tempRoot := filepath.Join(tempParent, "ddx-exec-wt")
	t.Setenv("DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR", tempRoot)
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName), 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(tempRoot, 0o755))
	return projectRoot, tempRoot
}

func writeCleanupCommandCandidate(t *testing.T, root, name, projectRoot, attemptID string) string {
	t.Helper()
	path := filepath.Join(root, name)
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(path, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, agent.WriteExecutionCleanupMetadata(path, agent.ExecutionCleanupMetadata{
		ProjectRoot:  projectRoot,
		BeadID:       "ddx-cleanup",
		AttemptID:    attemptID,
		WorktreePath: path,
	}))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(path, "scratch.txt"), []byte("stale\n"), 0o644))
	return path
}

func runCleanupCommandGit(t *testing.T, dir string, args ...string) string {
	t.Helper()
	cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
	cmd.Dir = dir
	out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, "git %s\n%s", strings.Join(args, " "), string(out))
	return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
}

func TestCleanupCommand_DryRunReportsWithoutDeleting(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot, tempRoot := setupCleanupCommandProject(t)
	stalePath := writeCleanupCommandCandidate(t, tempRoot, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"ddx-cleanup-dryrun-20260506T154739-deadbeef", projectRoot, "20260506T154739-deadbeef")
	runStatePath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, agent.RunStateFileName)
	require.NoError(t, agent.WriteRunState(projectRoot, agent.RunState{
		BeadID:       "ddx-cleanup",
		AttemptID:    "20260506T154739-live-feedface",
		StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
		WorktreePath: filepath.Join(tempRoot, "missing-live-path"),
	}))

	root := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(root, "cleanup")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Contains(t, out, "cleanup: would remove 1 stale temp dir(s), 0 registered worktree(s), 1 run-state file(s)")
	assert.Contains(t, out, "run again with --apply")
	assert.DirExists(t, stalePath)
	assert.FileExists(t, runStatePath)
}

func TestCleanupCommand_ApplyRemovesStaleDDxResources(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot, tempRoot := setupCleanupCommandProject(t)
	stalePath := writeCleanupCommandCandidate(t, tempRoot, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"ddx-cleanup-apply-20260506T154739-feedface", projectRoot, "20260506T154739-feedface")
	runStatePath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, agent.RunStateFileName)
	require.NoError(t, agent.WriteRunState(projectRoot, agent.RunState{
		BeadID:       "ddx-cleanup",
		AttemptID:    "20260506T154739-live-cafebabe",
		StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
		WorktreePath: filepath.Join(tempRoot, "missing-live-path"),
	}))
	evidenceDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, "executions", "attempt-complete")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(evidenceDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "manifest.json"), []byte(`{"attempt_id":"attempt-complete"}`), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "result.json"), []byte(`{"status":"success"}`), 0o644))

	root := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(root, "cleanup", "--apply")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Contains(t, out, "cleanup: removed 1 stale temp dir(s), 0 registered worktree(s), 1 run-state file(s)")
	assert.NoFileExists(t, stalePath)
	assert.NoFileExists(t, runStatePath)
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "manifest.json"))
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "result.json"))
}

func TestCleanupRunStateDoesNotDirtyTrackedRepo(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot, tempRoot := setupCleanupCommandProject(t)
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "init", "-b", "main")
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "add", "seed.txt")
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "commit", "-m", "chore: seed cleanup repo")

	ddxDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName)
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "run-state"), 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "run-state.json"), []byte(`{"attempt_id":"tracked-root"}`+"\n"), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "run-state", "tracked-attempt.json"), []byte(`{"attempt_id":"tracked-attempt"}`+"\n"), 0o644))
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "add", ".ddx/run-state.json", ".ddx/run-state/tracked-attempt.json")
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "commit", "-m", "test: track legacy run-state")

	root := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand()
	_, err := executeCommand(root, "init")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Empty(t, runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "ls-files", "--", ".ddx/run-state.json", ".ddx/run-state"))

	require.NoError(t, agent.WriteRunState(projectRoot, agent.RunState{
		BeadID:       "ddx-cleanup",
		AttemptID:    "20260506T154739-live-cafebabe",
		StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
		WorktreePath: filepath.Join(tempRoot, "missing-live-path"),
	}))
	attemptPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, agent.RunStateDirName, "20260506T154739-live-cafebabe.json")
	require.FileExists(t, attemptPath)

	out, err := executeCommand(root, "cleanup", "--apply")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Contains(t, out, "cleanup: removed 0 stale temp dir(s), 0 registered worktree(s), 2 run-state file(s), 0 scratch dir(s)")
	assert.NoFileExists(t, filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, agent.RunStateFileName))
	assert.NoFileExists(t, attemptPath)
	assert.Empty(t, runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "status", "--short", "--", ".ddx/run-state.json", ".ddx/run-state"),
		"cleanup must not leave tracked run-state deletions behind after migration")
}

func TestCleanupCommand_JSONShape(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot, tempRoot := setupCleanupCommandProject(t)
	writeCleanupCommandCandidate(t, tempRoot, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"ddx-cleanup-json-20260506T154739-12345678", projectRoot, "20260506T154739-12345678")
	require.NoError(t, agent.WriteRunState(projectRoot, agent.RunState{
		BeadID:       "ddx-cleanup",
		AttemptID:    "20260506T154739-live-90abcdef",
		StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
		WorktreePath: filepath.Join(tempRoot, "missing-live-path"),
	}))

	root := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(root, "cleanup", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var report cleanupCommandJSON
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &report))

	assert.True(t, report.DryRun)
	assert.Equal(t, int64(1), report.RemovedUnregisteredTempDirs)
	assert.Equal(t, int64(1), report.RemovedRunStateFiles)
	assert.NotEmpty(t, report.Warnings)
	assert.NotNil(t, report.BlockedErrors)
	assert.NotNil(t, report.Observations)
	assert.Greater(t, report.BytesReclaimed, int64(0))
	assert.Greater(t, report.InodesReclaimed, int64(0))
}

func TestCleanupCommand_DoesNotRemovePreservedEvidence(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot, tempRoot := setupCleanupCommandProject(t)
	stalePath := writeCleanupCommandCandidate(t, tempRoot, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"ddx-cleanup-preserved-20260506T154739-abcdef12", projectRoot, "20260506T154739-abcdef12")
	preservedPath := writeCleanupCommandCandidate(t, tempRoot, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"ddx-cleanup-preserved-20260506T154739-34567890", projectRoot, "20260506T154739-34567890")
	require.NoError(t, agent.WriteExecutionCleanupMetadata(preservedPath, agent.ExecutionCleanupMetadata{
		ProjectRoot:  projectRoot,
		BeadID:       "ddx-cleanup",
		AttemptID:    "20260506T154739-34567890",
		WorktreePath: preservedPath,
		Preserved:    true,
	}))
	evidenceDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, "executions", "attempt-complete")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(evidenceDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "manifest.json"), []byte(`{"attempt_id":"attempt-complete"}`), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "result.json"), []byte(`{"status":"success"}`), 0o644))
	runsDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, "runs", "run-complete")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(runsDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(runsDir, "record.json"), []byte(`{"run_id":"run-complete"}`), 0o644))

	root := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(root, "cleanup", "--apply")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Contains(t, out, "cleanup: removed 1 stale temp dir(s), 0 registered worktree(s), 0 run-state file(s)")
	assert.Contains(t, out, "cleanup: preserved 2 complete evidence bundle(s)")
	assert.NoFileExists(t, stalePath)
	assert.DirExists(t, preservedPath)
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "manifest.json"))
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "result.json"))
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(runsDir, "record.json"))
}

func TestCleanupEndToEnd_PreservesPublishedEvidence(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot, tempRoot := setupCleanupCommandProject(t)
	stalePath := writeCleanupCommandCandidate(t, tempRoot, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"ddx-cleanup-published-20260508T120000-abcdef12", projectRoot, "20260508T120000-abcdef12")

	executionsDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, "executions", "attempt-published")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(executionsDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(executionsDir, "manifest.json"), []byte(`{"attempt_id":"attempt-published"}`), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(executionsDir, "result.json"), []byte(`{"status":"success"}`), 0o644))

	runsDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, "runs", "run-published")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(runsDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(runsDir, "record.json"), []byte(`{"run_id":"run-published"}`), 0o644))

	refPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".git", "refs", "ddx", "iterations", "ddx-published", "attempt-1")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(refPath), 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(refPath, []byte("abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef12\n"), 0o644))

	root := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(root, "cleanup", "--apply")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.NoFileExists(t, stalePath)
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(executionsDir, "manifest.json"))
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(executionsDir, "result.json"))
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(runsDir, "record.json"))
	assert.FileExists(t, refPath)
	assert.Contains(t, out, "cleanup: preserved 2 complete evidence bundle(s)")
}

func TestWorkCleanupEndToEnd_PrunesStaleRegisteredAndUnregisteredWorktrees(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot, tempRoot := setupCleanupCommandProject(t)
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "init", "-b", "main")
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))

That cache sweep went too broad and hit immutable module-cache contents, so I’m stopping it. The safer path is to isolate the test temp root with `TMPDIR` instead of trying to rewrite the host cache in place.

data.text: The guard is only keyed off the command’s working directory and `DDX_EXECUTE_BEAD_ID`, so the failure means the harness is still pointing `ddx init` at a directory the code sees as an execute-bead worktree. I’m checking the test environment wiring before I decide whether this is fixable in-process or just out of scope for this bead.

The harness itself is clean; the failing `ddx init` calls are likely coming from commands launched with the wrong CWD rather than from the temp root. I’m checking the `RunCommand` helper next, because if it doesn’t chdir into the test project, that would explain the execute-bead guard failures across many acceptance tests.

`RunCommand` does use the test project dir, so the guard is tripping on path detection rather than a bad harness CWD. I’m reading the execute-bead worktree detector next, because that decides whether these `ddx init` failures are just environment constraints or a code path I can avoid without changing scope.
